rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Deposition Testimony; Supplemental Interrogatory Responses; Supplemental Production Of Documents And Things And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 23, 2013, Line 2. Defendants CELIA MORENO and COUNTY OF SAN MATEO'S Motion to Compel Deposition Testimony; Supplemental Interrogatory Responses; Supplemental Production of Documents and Things and for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part. Plaintiff must provide supplemental responses to form interrogatories (set 1), special interrogatories (sets 1 and 2), request for production of documents (sets 1 and 2) by June 1, 2013 pursuant to C.C.P. 2030.090, et seq, and 2031.090, et seq. The request for an additional deposition of Salma Khan is denied. It was not requested in the Notice and is therefore procedurally improper. The sanction request is granted in the amount of $1,000. Moving party should bring a Proposed Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COALITION FOR ADEQUATE REVIEW VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 23, 2013, Line 4, Order To Show Cause Re Contempt. Petitioner Coalition for Adequate Review's motion to hold Defendant City and County of San Francisco in contempt is denied. Petitioner has failed to establish the necessary elements for a showing of contempt because Respondent has not willfully disobeyed a Court order. See Conn v. Superior Court (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 774, 784. rulings.Case Title: MARK A. BROWN VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Adminstrativ Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 23, 2013, Line 7, PETITIONER MARK BROWN's Motion For Writ Of Adminstrativ Mandate. Granted. Respondents are ordered to award Petitioner the unemployment insurance benefits withheld plus interest on those benefits. Petitioner did not engage in misconduct to disqualify him for unemployment compensation benefits. (Umemp. Ins. Code § 1256; Amador v. Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (1984) 35 Cal.3d 671, 678.) The instant action is distinguishable from McRae v. CUIAB, where the petitioner refused to follow an employer policy. (McCrae v. California Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (1973) 30 Cal.App.3d 89.) Here, Petitioner attempted to correct his mistake by going home to change shirts. (AR00024, AR00044, AR00065 - AR00066.) Lastly, Petitioner was terminated on the first instance of Petitioner's violation of the employer's uniform policy after receipt of the custom sized uniform shirts. (Robles v. Employment Development Dept. (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 1029, 1035-36. See AR00030.) EDD's request for dismissal is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL LONG VS. SAN FRANCISCO 49ERS LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 23, 2013, Line 5. Plaintiff's ANDREW MEEHAN'S Motion To Compel Responses to Interrogatories and Request for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part. San Francisco 49ers Ltd. ("defendant") must provide a complete and straightforward answer to Interrogatory No. 12. The response was not objected to on relevance grounds and is thus not timely raised. The response can be qualified with an explanation but must be responded to in full and the improper objection that the question "assumes facts not in evidence" should be removed. As for Interrogatory No. 13, defendant must include a statement that "after a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information" it does not have data sufficient to respond to the interrogatory." Plaintiff should stipulate that "weapon" means "Things designed to inflict bodily harm." Interrogatory No. 13 should also contain the language that a reasonable and good faith effort was made to obtain the information." The motion is denied as to Interrogatory No. 15. Sanctions are denied as the Court finds substantial justification by the Defendant in responding as it did. Moving party shall provide a proposed order tracking the tentative ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PAGE VS. MICHAEL WORNICK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs Per Ccp 425.16(C) And This Court'S Order Of April 11, 2013
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 23, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTs COLONY INSURANCE COMPANY and COLONY SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs Per Ccp 425.16(C) And This Court'S Order Of April 11, 2013. Continued to June 20, 2013 on the Court's own motion to be heard by Judge Miller. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: BRAD D SANDERS VS. EDWARD ZUSMAN, AN INDIVIDUAL ATTORNEY LICENSED TO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 23, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTs EDWARD ZUSMAN, AN INDIVIDUAL ATTORNEY LICENSED TO practice law and MARKUN, ZUSMAN, AND COMPTON, LLP's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The demurrers to causes of action 1-3 are overruled. The Court cannot conclude as a matter of law that the actual injury and continuous representation tolling provisions of CCP 340.6 do not apply to this action. The Court sustains the demurrer to the fourth cause of action for fraud with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave only to clarify claimed misrepresentations of fact by defendants and facts to demonstrate plaintiff's reliance thereon. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBIN MATTHEWS VS. KNOWLEDGE NATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder And Joinder To Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 23, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT ENTELLECT LIMITED's Joinder To Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration. The Petition for Order Compelling Arbitration is granted. The scope of the disputes in the First Amended Complaint are all within the scope of the arbitration clause. (Par. 15 of the Executive Employment Agreement dated as of April 1, 2012) ("Par. 15"). The Plaintiff has not met his burden of proof to demonstrate a legally justifiable basis for not enforcing the arbitration clause. Plaintiff has not met his burden of proof that the arbitration clause was a condition of his employment, so the costs of arbitration are to be allocated in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association and Par. 15. See Armendariz v. Foundation Health Care (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. Pursuant to Par. 15, each Petitioner is entitled to its attorney's fees and costs incurred in this action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)
